features.summary: NVIDIA reported record Q1 fiscal 2025 revenue of $26 billion, up 18% sequentially and 262% year-over-year, driven primarily by Data Center revenue of $22.6 billion (up 427% YoY). Strong demand for Hopper GPU computing platform continues across cloud providers, enterprises, and consumer internet companies. The company is transitioning to H200 production in Q2 and Blackwell platform shipments starting Q2 with customer deployments in Q4. Gaming revenue was $2.65 billion, down 8% sequentially but up 18% YoY, while other segments showed mixed performance.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 revenue of $28 billion with sequential growth across all platforms. Blackwell is in full production with significant revenue expected this year, though demand may exceed supply well into next year. The company anticipates automotive to become the largest enterprise vertical within Data Center, and Sovereign AI revenue could approach high single-digit billions this year. Full-year gross margins expected in mid-70s percent range with OpEx growing in low 40% range.
features.risks: Supply constraints continue for H200 and Blackwell products with demand significantly outpacing supply. China revenue remains substantially lower due to export control restrictions and increased competition in that market. The rapid pace of product transitions creates potential customer timing decisions between current and next-generation platforms. Liquid cooling deployment at scale presents engineering challenges for both NVIDIA and customers that could impact Blackwell rollout timing.
features.key_drivers: Accelerated computing platform shift from traditional data centers to AI factories is driving fundamental industry transformation. Strong inference workload growth (40% of Data Center revenue) alongside continued training demand expansion. Spectrum-X Ethernet solution opening new markets for large-scale AI in Ethernet-only data centers, expected to reach multibillion-dollar revenue within a year. NVIDIA Inference Microservices (NIMs) launching as new software offering for enterprise-grade AI deployment across cloud and on-premises environments.
